processed.review_note: Source confirmed (energy-storage.news): Pacific Green sells 250MW/500MWh Limestone Coast North BESS, SA, to Intera Renewables (Palisade-managed) for A$460m (US$293m), 21 Feb 2025, operational 2027. All figures match. EV/MWh ~A$920k is high for 2h but reasonable as a marquee-asset enterprise value. High confidence justified by primary trade source.
processed.name: Palisade (Intera) / Limestone Coast North BESS
processed.notes: A$460m enterprise value (US$293m) for a 250 MW/500 MWh BESS in SA's Limestone Coast region. First large-scale BESS acquisition for Palisade/Intera. Phase 1 of Pacific Green's 500MW/1.5GWh Limestone Coast Energy Park. Expected operational by early 2027.

metrics.notes: A$460m EV at financial close, Feb 2025. ~A$1.84m/MW and ~A$920k/MWh.

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (pe-insights): KKR buys Zenith Energy from PEP/OPTrust/Foresight for A$1.7bn (~US$1.1bn), 710MW across ~15 WA/NT off-grid sites, ~12x contracted earnings, close late 2025. Figures match. EV is media-reported — Medium confidence correctly applied. A$2.4m/MW sane for off-grid IPP.
processed.name: KKR / Zenith Energy (Australia)
processed.seller: Pacific Equity Partners, OPTrust, Foresight Group
processed.notes: A$1.7bn (USD ~1.1bn) deal for Australia's leading off-grid IPP. ~710 MW contracted capacity across ~15 remote/microgrid sites in WA and NT. Zenith completed A$1.9bn debt refinancing. Founder/management retain minority. Close expected late 2025. EV not officially confirmed — Bloomberg/media figure.

metrics.notes: A$1.7bn — media-reported figure (Bloomberg); not officially disclosed by KKR. ~A$2.4m/MW on 710 MW.

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (Squadron Energy): A$1bn debt, 10 capital partners, financial close 4 Dec 2025, ~450MW, record single-asset wind financing. Figures match. CAUTION: source phrases capacity as '450MW (Stage 1); combined Stages 1+2 exceed 1GW' — the named stage is ambiguous; the financing milestone and figures are solid regardless. PPA tenor (20yr) is a Low-confidence analogy, correctly flagged.
processed.name: Clarke Creek Wind Farm Stage 2 (Squadron Energy)
processed.notes: A$1bn debt facility (10-bank syndicate, financial close 4 Dec 2025) for Stage 2 of Clarke Creek Wind Farm (~450 MW). Combined Stages 1+2 exceed 1 GW — largest single-asset wind financing in Australian market history. Stage 1 (also ~450 MW) already operating. No capex or PPA price disclosed.

metrics.notes: A$1bn from 10 banks — record single-asset wind financing in Australia. ~A$2.2m/MW of debt.
CS Energy 20-yr PPA on Stage 1 Boulder Creek (comparable project structure); Clarke Creek offtake terms not publicly disclosed.

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (pv magazine AU): A$830m (US$553m) debt from 7 named banks, 11 Dec 2025, supporting >600MW new projects + 800+MW operating. Figures match exactly. Distinct from existing 'Potentia Energy / 1GW+ AU portfolio' acquisition (Apr 2025) — this is a financing event, not a duplicate. High confidence justified.
processed.name: Potentia Energy portfolio financing (Enel/Inpex)
processed.notes: A$830m portfolio debt from 7 banks (Bank of China, BNP Paribas, HSBC, Mizuho, SocGen, SMBC, Westpac) secured Dec 2025. Covers 800+ MW of existing wind/solar/hybrid assets (Bungala 275MW, Clare 128MW, Girgarre 93MW, Cohuna 34MW, Royalla 23MW, Greenough River 40MW, Quorn Park 98MW + BESS). National portfolio — individual per-MW benchmarks are blended.

metrics.notes: Portfolio debt facility across 800+ MW operating assets. ~A$1.04m/MW blended debt (solar-heavy).

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (RenewEconomy): 228MW, A$740m, 38 turbines, 50/50 Aula/CS Energy JV, CS Energy 20yr PPA for all output, ~40km SW Rockhampton, FC late Sep 2024. All figures match. A$3.25m/MW is bang-on GenCost 2024-25 onshore wind (A$3.22m/MW) — excellent calibration.
processed.name: Boulder Creek Wind Farm (Aula / CS Energy)
processed.notes: ~A$740m total project cost (38 GE Vernova turbines, 228 MW), ~40 km SW of Rockhampton QLD. Financial close 27 Sep 2024. CS Energy holds 100% PPA (20-yr); JV 50/50 ownership. Debt from CBA, NAB, ANZ, ING. Construction began Nov 2024; commercial operations 2027. Aula Energy is a Macquarie-backed vehicle.

metrics.notes: CS Energy 20-yr PPA for 100% of output.

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (RenewEconomy): 288MW SA, 40 Vestas V172-7.2MW, FC 31 Dec 2025, CIS Tender 4 award, AGL long-term PPA, ~70km east of Adelaide. All figures match. No capex metric asserted; PPA tenor (15yr) is a Low-confidence estimate, correctly flagged.
processed.name: Palmer Wind Farm (Tilt Renewables)
processed.notes: 288 MW SA wind farm (40 Vestas V172-7.2 MW turbines — most powerful in AU), ~70 km east of Adelaide. FID/financial close 31 Dec 2025. CIS Tender 4 award. AGL long-term PPA. Construction mid-2026; commissioning late 2027. Total project cost not disclosed; part of Tilt's A$2.25bn corporate refinancing.

metrics.notes: AGL PPA — 'long-term'; 15yr is estimated comparable to Waddi (same developer). Term not publicly disclosed.

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (Tilt Renewables): 108MW WA ~150km N of Perth, AGL 15yr PPA, FID Dec 2025 (first AU wind FID of 2025), A$3.9m community benefit over life, ops 2028. Capacity/PPA/community all match. Capex A$400m is NOT in the source (media estimate) — correctly flagged Low confidence.
processed.name: Waddi Wind Farm (Tilt Renewables)
processed.notes: 108 MW WA wind farm (~150 km north of Perth), first wind FID in Australia in 2025. AGL 15-yr PPA. Vestas turbines. Construction 2026; operations 2028. Estimated capex ≥A$400m per media (US$265m). Part of Tilt's A$850m dual-tranche syndicated facility. Community benefit A$3.9m over life.

metrics.notes: ~A$400m media estimate (A$3.7m/MW); part of Tilt's A$850m portfolio facility — project-level capex not separately disclosed.
A$3.9m total over project life (~25yr); ~A$156k/yr average.

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (RenewEconomy): 150MW/600MWh (4h) TagEnergy BESS at Golden Plains site VIC, FC Dec 2025, lenders Westpac/Bank of China/Siemens FS, Snowy Hydro virtual toll, 168 Tesla Megapack 2XL, ops mid-2027. All figures match. Distinct asset from existing 'Golden Plains Wind Farm' (same site) — not a duplicate. PPA tenor (15yr) is a Low-confidence analogy, correctly flagged.
processed.name: Golden Plains BESS (TagEnergy)
processed.notes: 150 MW/600 MWh (4h) BESS at Golden Plains Wind Farm site, VIC. Financial close Dec 2025. Lenders: Westpac, Bank of China, Siemens Financial Services. Long-term virtual tolling agreement with Snowy Hydro for most of capacity. Tesla 168 Megapack 2XL; EPC by CPP. Commissioning mid-2027. Project cost not disclosed.

metrics.notes: Snowy Hydro virtual tolling — term not disclosed. 15yr is estimate by analogy with Akaysha Elaine (same structure, same counterparty).

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (The Urban Developer): 550MW NEXTDC sovereign AI campus at Eastern Creek, A$7bn investment, site bought ~A$353m (2024), OpenAI anchor under 'OpenAI for Countries', first phase H2 2027. All figures match. A$7bn capex and 550MW both media/announcement figures — Medium confidence appropriate.
processed.name: NEXTDC S7 / OpenAI Eastern Creek campus
processed.notes: 550 MW sovereign AI campus at Eastern Creek, Western Sydney. A$7bn total investment. OpenAI is anchor offtaker under MOU (OpenAI for Countries program). NEXTDC acquired the site in 2024 for ~A$353m. First phase expected H2 2027 (subject to approvals). Closed-loop liquid cooling; powered by long-term renewable PPAs. Exceeds CDC's 504 MW facility as largest planned in Southern Hemisphere.

metrics.notes: A$7bn total investment — media-reported figure. Includes land (A$353m site) + construction.
550 MW IT load capacity per The Urban Developer.

processed.review_note: Source confirmed (Microsoft newsroom): A$25bn (US$18bn) to expand Azure AI infra by >140% by end-2029, announced 23 Apr 2026 by Nadella + PM Albanese; covers infra, cybersecurity, skills, AI safety. All figures match primary source. Multi-year capex commitment (no MW) — correctly framed as a hyperscaler-scale benchmark; High confidence justified by first-party source.
processed.name: Microsoft Australia AI infrastructure commitment
processed.notes: A$25bn (USD$18bn) capital commitment to expand Australia's Azure AI cloud infrastructure by >140% by end-2029. Announced 23 Apr 2026 by Satya Nadella + PM Albanese. Covers cybersecurity, AI supercomputing and workforce training. Existing footprint 29 sites across 3 Azure regions. MW capacity not disclosed — capex-only benchmark for hyperscaler infra scale in AU.

metrics.notes: Multi-year capex 2026–2029; not a single-asset figure. Useful as a hyperscaler-commitment scale benchmark alongside AWS A$20bn.

processed.review_note: Source (Partners Group press release, 6 Mar 2025) confirms up to AUD 1.2bn to transform GreenSquareDC; first Sydney site expandable to 96 MW; land options across Sydney/Melbourne/Perth. Notes correctly flag 96 MW = single-site expansion and 1.2bn = platform commitment, so $/MW is indicative only. Not a duplicate of existing entries. Confirmed IT load: 96 MW (site expansion target).
processed.name: Partners Group / GreenSquareDC
processed.notes: Partners Group to invest up to AUD 1.2bn to transform GreenSquareDC into a hyperscale/AI data centre platform across Sydney, Melbourne and Perth. First Sydney site is a brownfield with powered shell + adjoining landbank enabling expansion to 96 MW of compute capacity. IT load (96 MW) is the disclosed site expansion capacity, not platform-wide; investment is a platform commitment not a single-asset EV, so $/MW from these two figures is indicative only.

processed.review_note: Source (AirTrunk primary) confirms MEL2 >354 MW capacity, >A$5bn direct investment, lifting Melbourne total to >630 MW (MEL1+MEL2 >A$7bn). Distinct from existing 'Blackstone+CPP/AirTrunk' M&A entry (that is the platform EV). Implied ~A$14m/MW capex is sane for a hyperscale build. Confirmed IT load: 354 MW. Approve.
processed.name: AirTrunk MEL2 (Melbourne campus)
processed.notes: AirTrunk's second Melbourne hyperscale campus (MEL2) in Melbourne's north-west: 354 MW IT load, more than A$5bn new direct investment. Lifts AirTrunk's total deployable Melbourne capacity to over 630 MW (MEL1+MEL2 combined >A$7bn). IT load 354 MW. Capex/MW derivable from $5bn / 354 MW.

processed.notes: Planning application filed for a 168 MW hyperscale data centre (twelve 14 MW data halls) at 90 Aldington Road, Kemps Creek, West Sydney; up to AU$3.94bn investment. 1 GW substation also planned to serve the wider industrial park. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling. IT load 168 MW.

processed.notes: NEXTDC received Victorian Government development approval for the M4 technology campus at Fishermans Bend, Port Melbourne (former Westgate Park Printing Complex, 127 Todd Road). 162 MW, A$2bn. processed.review_note: Source (DCD) confirms Project Berlin: 850 MW S4+S7 Western Sydney, A$15bn over 10+ years, advisors Morgan Stanley + Cadence, NEXTDC retains minority/operates, FC 12-18 months. processed.name: NEXTDC Project Berlin — Western Sydney 850MW JV (S4/S7)
processed.notes: NEXTDC seeking a JV partner (Project Berlin; advisors Morgan Stanley + Cadence Advisory) for 850 MW of new data-centre capacity in Western Sydney (S4 + S7), AU$15bn over 10+ years. NEXTDC to retain minority stake and operate. Financial close targeted 12-18 months. IT load 850 MW (planned aggregate). Capex is a long-dated programme figure, not single-asset EV.

processed.review_note: Source (DCNN) confirms IC3 Super West topped out at Macquarie Park: 47 MW total planned capacity, A$350m PHASE 1 investment delivering core+shell with 6 MW IT load fitted, opening Sep 2026. CORRECTED: the A$350m is Phase-1-only (source explicitly ties it to the 6 MW fitted), so capex must be read against the 6 MW fitted load, NOT the 47 MW total — do not derive $/MW from 350/47. Candidate correctly carries both it_load (47 MW total planned) and contracted_load (6 MW Phase-1 fitted). Capex note amended to flag Phase-1 scope. Confirmed IT load: 47 MW total planned / 6 MW fitted Phase 1. Approve.
processed.name: Macquarie Data Centres — IC3 Super West (Macquarie Park, Sydney)
processed.notes: Macquarie Data Centres topped out IC3 Super West at its Macquarie Park (Sydney) campus: 47 MW total planned capacity (6 MW IT load fitted in Phase 1), ~A$350m investment, purpose-built for high-density AI / liquid cooling. Opening Q3 2026. IT load: 47 MW total planned; capacity_mw = 47 MW total, contracted/fitted Phase-1 load = 6 MW.

metrics.notes: Phase-1 investment only (core+shell with 6 MW fitted); not the full 47 MW build-out. Do not derive $/MW against 47 MW.
Total planned capacity; only 6 MW fitted in Phase 1 (see contracted_load).

processed.review_note: Source verifies 430 MW of renewable GENERATION across 9 PPAs (NSW/VIC), not data-centre IT load. Two problems: (1) the only hard money metric (total_project_cost 20000 = A$20bn) duplicates the existing 'AWS Australia data-centre investment' deal already in comps-scrape.json (same A$20bn 2025-29 commitment); (2) capacity_mw=430 is generation, not IT/critical load, and contracted_pct=100% is unsupported. This is a generation-PPA story mislabelled as a data-centre comp with a duplicated capex figure. No new, correctly-labelled data-centre metric. Reject.
processed.name: Amazon (AWS) Australia data-centre renewable PPAs
processed.notes: Amazon signed nine renewable PPAs totalling 430 MW of generation capacity (1 wind 201.8MW, 3 utility solar-plus-storage, 4 distributed solar-plus-storage, 1 battery add-on) across NSW and VIC to support data-centre/AI expansion. Underpins AWS's AU$20bn commitment to expand AU data-centre infrastructure by 2029. NOTE: 430 MW is renewable GENERATION capacity contracted, NOT data-centre IT load (no IT load MW disclosed); capacity_mw here = contracted generation, not IT critical load.

processed.review_note: Source confirms Brooklyn ~A$2bn estimated value, but the 350 MW is power SECURED across TWO Melbourne sites (Brooklyn + Cherry Lane, Laverton North) — not Brooklyn-only IT load. capacity_mw=350 and it_load=350 are misattributed to the single Brooklyn site, and pairing the Brooklyn-only A$2bn capex against a two-site 350 MW load yields a meaningless ~A$5.7m/MW $/MW comp. Brooklyn-only IT load is not disclosed, so the load cannot be cleanly stated. Unverifiable as a single-asset benchmark — reject rather than guess. Confirmed IT load: not separable for Brooklyn (350 MW is a two-site allocation).
processed.name: Stockland — Brooklyn (Melbourne)
processed.notes: Stockland data-centre planning application at 413 Francis Street, Brooklyn (Melbourne), ~A$2bn estimated end value. Source states the 350 MW IT load is shared across two Melbourne sites (Brooklyn + Cherry Lane, Laverton North), so 350 MW is a combined-allocation figure, not necessarily Brooklyn-only — treat IT load with caution. Under Development Facilitation Program review by VIC Dept of Transport and Planning.
